text.title: Fine-tune vs prompt economic model — core rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every economic model artefact must satisfy.

rule.statement: `ops_overhead_per_month` is set to a positive number (USD), including on-call, monitoring, A/B routing, model maintenance. Zero is never accepted.
rule.rationale: Modeling FT without ops overhead is the single most common reason real bills overshoot the spreadsheet 2-3x.

rule.statement: Four scenarios MUST be present: `base`, `volume_minus_50`, `volume_plus_50`, `prompt_cost_minus_30_eoy`. Each runs the full model with its inputs perturbed.
rule.rationale: Single-scenario models give the team one number; reality lives in the distribution around it.

rule.statement: A sensitivity table reports break-even month under ±20% volume, ±20% $/k tokens, ±50% ops overhead. Cells where break-even > 36 months are explicitly marked "never".
rule.rationale: Knowing which input matters most lets the team focus monitoring on the right number.

rule.statement: `vendor_risk_premium_pct` (default 10–20%) is applied to FT side as an additional monthly carry, to cover vendor-shutdown / model-deprecation risk.
rule.rationale: FT locks you to a vendor's model lifecycle; the cost of vendor change is real and recurring.

rule.statement: Outputs include both `monthly_delta_usd` AND `npv_12mo_usd` (sum of monthly deltas, no discounting required at the granularity used). `break_even_month` is computed (or set to "never").
rule.rationale: One-number summaries hide cumulative profile; teams that look only at month-1 lose money for 4 months and abandon FT.

rule.statement: Token units are normalised: `input_tokens_per_day` and `output_tokens_per_day` separately, both at observed split. Mixing input/output rates is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Output tokens cost 3–5× input on most providers; conflating them flips the FT decision direction.

rule.statement: All six rules satisfied: ops_overhead > 0, 4 scenarios, sensitivity table, vendor risk premium, monthly+NPV+break-even, units split.
No production traffic, <$500/mo spend, or vendor lock-step pricing — model has no signal.
